content:
# Spoke Observability: Use corvia-telemetry, Not corvia_write (2026-03-28)

Three review items (M1 docker stats, M8 audit log, M9 spoke log capture) are unified under corvia-telemetry instead of separate mechanisms.

## Why telemetry, not knowledge
Spoke metrics, lifecycle events, and log capture are operational signals, not knowledge. They do not belong in the knowledge store because they would pollute search results and consume embedding compute. corvia-telemetry already has the pipeline: structured tracing -> exporters (stdout, file, OTLP) -> dashboard Traces/Logs views.

## Implementation
- Spoke container metrics: OTel gauge metrics (spoke.cpu_percent, spoke.memory_bytes) emitted by hub SpokeProvisioner on 15s poll
- Lifecycle audit: tracing::info_span!("spoke.lifecycle") with structured fields (spoke_name, repo, issue, action) on create/destroy
- Spoke log capture: Claude Code output streamed via docker logs API, ingested as tracing::info! events tagged with spoke.name
- Add spoke.* span names to corvia-telemetry span name constants
- Dashboard Traces tab already renders these. No new UI needed.
